title: The Clockmaker's House
description: The protagonist is trapped in a mysterious clockmaker's house where every room is a puzzle. Tests logic reasoning, object interaction, cause-and-effect chains, and spatial memory.
setting: A Victorian-era house belonging to a reclusive clockmaker who has disappeared. Every room contains mechanical puzzles and hidden mechanisms. The front door locked behind the protagonist.

protagonist.name: Jules
protagonist.description: A curious locksmith invited by letter to the clockmaker's house. Practical, observant, good with mechanical things.

turns.user_input: Look around the foyer. What do I see?
Read the clockmaker's letter again for any clues
Examine the grandfather clock in the foyer
Check what time my pocket watch shows versus the grandfather clock
Try to adjust the hands on the grandfather clock
Something clicked. What changed in the room?
Go through the door that just opened
I'm in a library. Examine the room carefully
Check the books on the desk. Any of them look important?
Open the book that stands out and read it
There are numbers written in the margins. Write them down
Look at the fireplace. Is there anything unusual about it?
Reach into the fireplace and feel around
I found a small metal key. Take it and examine it
Are there any locked drawers or cabinets in the library?
Try the key on the locked cabinet
What's inside the cabinet?
Take the gear mechanism and examine it closely
Check if this gear fits anywhere in this room
Go back to the foyer and try the gear on the grandfather clock
Insert the gear. What happens?
A new passage opened. Where does it lead?
Enter the workshop. Describe everything I see
Examine the large mechanical device on the workbench
It's missing three components. What shapes are the empty slots?
Search the workshop for any of the missing components
Found one under the workbench. Take it. Check my inventory
There's a door to a garden. Go outside
The garden has a sundial in the center. Examine it
The sundial has symbols matching the ones from the book. What do they mean?
Try using the numbers from the book margins on the sundial
A compartment in the sundial opens. What's inside?
Take the second component. Now where's the third?
Go back inside and check rooms I haven't fully explored
There's a staircase I missed. Go upstairs
The upstairs hallway has three doors. Check each one
Enter the first unlocked room
It's a bedroom with a music box. Open the music box
The melody seems important. Does it match anything I've seen?
Try the second door
This room has a combination lock on a chest. Use the numbers from the book
Open the chest. What's inside?
The third component! Take it. Check my full inventory now
Go back to the workshop and place all three components in the device
The device activates. What does it do?
Follow the mechanical sequence. Where does it lead me?
I've found a hidden basement room. What's down here?
Examine the clockmaker's final creation
There's a message from the clockmaker. Read it
Activate the final mechanism and leave the house
